issues: generic-rpg-card risk: ornate border reads as standard fantasy RPG rather than distinctly fairy-tale/Hyrule whimsy
interior fill too plain/white — lacks warmth or texture that would read 'toy-diorama parchment'
top centerpiece element ambiguous — crown silhouette is on-tone but pixelation makes it hard to confirm charm register
reasoning: Candidate (right, 4x objective card): warm cream/gold palette is appropriate and avoids grimdark or cold-corporate drift. Parchment background on the outer canvas is reassuring. The card border uses golden ornamental trim that skews toward generic-RPG rather than the specific Zelda/fairy-tale register — it is not grimdark or edgy, but lacks the playful toybox warmth of the target. Interior is near-white and visually flat, which reads as placeholder-ish rather than inviting parchment. The peer reference tileset (left) contains blood-stained floors and bone debris — those tiles do lean dark-dungeon-crawler, but they are the reference context, not the candidate; the candidate card itself shows no such drift. No fan-service, occult, corporate, or uncanny signals detected. Net: warm enough to avoid a fail, but insufficiently whimsical/Hyrule-specific to pass cleanly.
